verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type 1 single-pass transmembrane protein residing primarily in the ER membrane, where it senses luminal Ca2+ depletion. Upon store depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM junctions, where it physically contacts and gates the plasma membrane Orai1/CRAC channels. During this activation, the cytoplasmic C-terminal SOAR/CAD domain reaches across to the PM, but crucially STIM1 itself also accumulates at ER-PM junctions with its single-pass TM architecture bridging across. Beyond its ER-junctional role, there is documented evidence that a pool of STIM1 traffics to the plasma membrane as a type I TM protein with an N-terminal extracellular domain exposed on the outer leaflet. Surface biotinylation and flow cytometry studies have detected STIM1 at the cell surface, where its luminal/extracellular EF-hand domain faces outside. This surface pool is enhanced during store depletion and in specific cell types. Thus STIM1 has its own TM anchor and documented PM surface pool with extracellular domain accessibility.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The PM surface pool of STIM1 is minor relative to its dominant ER localization; whether this constitutes stable baseline surface display or is predominantly contextual/activation-induced is debated.
